text: The article tries to answer the question: How do international systems of modernization of national professional education systems act through implementing the Bologna and Copenhagen processes? By formalizing and systemizing the integral components of international integration for national education systems – ensuring competitiveness at the world education market, modernization of the structure and content of professional education, reform of education management and state regulation, and quality assurance in education – the authors look into the globalisation mechanisms for national education systems through standardisation of education and into the globalisation challenges for the national system in Russia. They estimate advantages and disadvantages of the Russian educational market including the base scientific schools and a reasonable pricing policy; problems with brain drain and the threat of pedagogical standardisation. They examine and give evidence of socio-economic influence on the host countries due to international students mobility and possible financial and work consequences for local economies that will help university management and international departments to implement strategic decisions for preparing professional educational institutions for joining international educational systems and to reduce negative consequences for teachers and educational institutions during the modernization reforms.
headline: Fully Human Written
